questions.stem: An armature controlled DC servo motor controls liquid level H in a tank via a gear train (ratio 1:5). Given: KA=10 V/V, KP=5 V/rad, KT=1 Nm/A, Kb=1 V/rad/s, Ra=1 Ω, Jv=12.5 kg.m², Bv=6.25 Nm/rad/s, Jm=0.5 kg.m², C=1, R=2, KV=0.5, Kf=1 rad/m. Draw the block diagram.
common_errors: Missing gear train reflection of inertia/friction to motor shaft
Incorrect tank dynamics (ignoring R, C in liquid level block)
Forgetting float linkage Kf in feedback path
questions.solution_method: Model each subsystem: error amplifier (KA, KP), DC motor armature (KT/(Ra(Jm_eff s + Bm_eff))), gear train (1/5 speed reduction, reflected inertia Jm_eff = Jm + Jv/25), valve (KV), tank (1/(Cs+1/R)), float feedback (Kf). Connect in cascade with unity feedback via Kf.
key_formulas: Motor TF: θm(s)/Ea(s) = KT / [s(Ra·J_eff·s + Ra·B_eff + Kb·KT)]

questions.stem: For the same liquid-level servo system, determine the transfer function ΔH(s)/Δθ_R(s).
common_errors: Arithmetic error in reflected inertia/friction
Dropping gear ratio N in valve angle relationship
questions.solution_method: Substitute numerical values into block diagram, reduce to single forward path and feedback. Apply Mason's or standard reduction: TF = G(s)/(1+G(s)H(s)) with G and H from part (a).

questions.stem: For the liquid-level servo system, determine whether the negative real part of the closed-loop poles are more negative than -1.
common_errors: Applying RH to un-shifted CE instead of substituting s = p-1
Sign error in shifted characteristic equation coefficients
questions.solution_method: Obtain closed-loop characteristic equation from part (b). Substitute s = (p-1) and apply Routh-Hurwitz to the resulting polynomial in p; if all entries in first column positive, all poles have real part < -1.
key_formulas: All first-column RH entries > 0 ⟹ all poles have Re(s) < -1

questions.stem: Tabulate the sequence of signals to rotate a permanent magnet stepper motor (coils A, B, C, D) in both directions by 90° in each step. Starting position: A & D ON, B & C OFF.
common_errors: Reversing CW vs CCW sequence direction
Incorrect step size (confusing full-step vs half-step)
questions.solution_method: For a 4-coil PM stepper with 90° per step: CW sequence cycles through (A,D ON), (A,B ON), (B,C ON), (C,D ON); CCW reverses. Tabulate 4 steps in each direction starting from initial state.
key_formulas: Step angle = 360°/number of rotor teeth (for PM stepper, 4-phase full-step = 90°/step)

questions.stem: Sketch the Nyquist plot for G(s)H(s) = K(s+3)(s+4) / [s(s-4)(s+5)], choosing the appropriate Nyquist contour. Determine the range of K (K>0) for closed-loop stability and the Gain Margin for K=1.
common_errors: Misidentifying P (open-loop RHP poles): s=4 is RHP pole, P=1
Wrong encirclement count due to RHP pole at origin indentation direction
Incorrect gain crossover for GM calculation
questions.solution_method: Note OLTF has one RHP pole (s=4) and one pole at origin: P=1. Indent Nyquist contour at s=0. Trace G(jω) for ω: 0+ to ∞, map semicircle at infinity, and mirror for negative ω. Count net encirclements N of -1+j0. Use N = Z - P → Z = N + 1; for stability Z=0 so N must equal -1 (one CCW encirclement). Find K range ensuring this. For GM at K=1: find ω where ∠G(jω)=-180°, compute |G(jω)| and GM = 1/|G(jω_pc)|.
key_formulas: N = Z - P (Z: CL RHP poles, P: OL RHP poles)

questions.stem: The Nyquist plot for a system with no open-loop RHP poles is shown (figure-8 shaped locus). Comment on closed-loop stability using Nyquist criterion when (i) point x is -1+j0 and (ii) point x' is -1+j0.
common_errors: Confusing clockwise vs counterclockwise encirclements for figure-8 plot
Forgetting P=0 so N=Z directly
questions.solution_method: P=0, so Z=N. For each critical point location, count net CW encirclements of that point by the Nyquist locus. If N=0, system is stable (Z=0); if N>0, unstable. Figure-8 locus means the two loops encircle in opposite senses — outer vs inner point gives different N.
key_formulas: Z = N + P, P = 0 → Z = N

questions.stem: For the system with OLTF K(1+a/s)·4/[(s+4)] and feedback 2/s, draw neat root contour sketches for a=3 and a=5, showing all steps (0 < K < ∞).
common_errors: Incorrect open-loop poles/zeros after block diagram reduction
Forgetting the 2/s inner feedback loop modifies the effective plant
Missing breakaway/break-in point calculation
questions.solution_method: First reduce the inner feedback loop (plant 4/(s+4) with feedback 2/s) to get equivalent forward TF. Then combine with controller K(1+a/s) = K(s+a)/s. Find OL poles and zeros, apply standard RL rules: asymptotes, centroid, breakaway, jω crossing. Sketch separately for a=3 and a=5.

questions.stem: For the same system, determine the range of 'a' for which the system is stable for all 0 < K < ∞.
common_errors: Not accounting for both K→0 and K→∞ stability limits
Forgetting inner loop poles when writing closed-loop CE
questions.solution_method: Write overall closed-loop characteristic equation in terms of K and a. Apply Routh-Hurwitz; all first-column elements must be positive for all K>0. Solve inequalities to find valid range of a.
key_formulas: RH stability: all first column entries > 0

questions.stem: For the same system, determine the steady-state error for an input of (t²/2)u(t).
common_errors: Miscounting system type (number of integrators in OL TF)
Using Kv instead of Ka for parabolic input
questions.solution_method: Determine system type by counting open-loop integrators. For parabolic input r(t)=t²/2 (R(s)=1/s³), ess = 1/Ka where Ka = lim s²→0 s²·G(s). If type < 2, error is infinite.

questions.stem: For the system with a=1.5, a certain K gives settling time (2% band) = 10 s and peak overshoot = 43% for the dominant pole pair. Determine K and the location of the third closed-loop pole.
common_errors: Using wrong ts formula (4/σ vs 4/ζωn)
Not verifying dominant pole assumption for third pole location
questions.solution_method: From Mp=43%: ζ = -ln(Mp/100)/√(π²+ln²(Mp/100)) ≈ 0.21. From ts=10 s (2% criterion): σ = ζωn = 4/ts = 0.4. Find ωn = σ/ζ. Locate dominant poles s = -σ ± jωd. Use CE with a=1.5 to find K from dominant poles, then obtain third pole by polynomial division or Vieta's formula.
key_formulas: Sum of closed-loop poles = -sum of CE coefficient (Vieta)

questions.stem: For G(s)H(s) = K(s+5) / [s²(s+50)], draw the asymptotic Bode magnitude and phase plots for K=80. Determine gain crossover frequency, phase crossover frequency, Gain Margin, and Phase Margin.
common_errors: Incorrect initial slope (-40 dB/dec for type-2) starting magnitude
Phase of double integrator (-180°) not included in total phase
Confusing gain crossover (|G|=1) with phase crossover (∠G=-180°)
questions.solution_method: Express in Bode standard form. Type-2 system: initial slope -40 dB/dec. Corner frequencies at ω=5 (zero, +20 dB/dec change) and ω=50 (pole, -20 dB/dec change). Compute 0 dB crossing (ωgc) and -180° crossing (ωpc). GM = -20log|G(jωpc)|, PM = 180° + ∠G(jωgc).
key_formulas: 20log K at ω=1 adjusted for double integrator: magnitude = 20log(K·5/50) at corner correction

questions.stem: For G(s)H(s) = K(s+5)/[s²(s+50)], determine the value of K for the system to have a phase margin of 45°.
common_errors: Not re-computing gain crossover frequency after K adjustment
Using phase margin formula at wrong frequency
questions.solution_method: Find ωgc where PM=45°: solve ∠G(jω)=-135°, i.e. arctan(ω/5)-arctan(ω/50)=45°. At that ωgc, set |G(jωgc)|=1 and solve for K.

questions.stem: The asymptotic Bode magnitude plot shows: initial slope 0 dB/dec rising to +20 dB/dec at ω=1, 20 dB magnitude at ω=1 to ω=10, then 0 dB/dec from ω=10 to ω=30, then -20 dB/dec and -40 dB/dec at ω=30. Determine the transfer function.
common_errors: Misreading slope changes as pole/zero at wrong frequency
Forgetting DC gain from 20 dB initial magnitude
Double pole vs two separate poles at ω=30
questions.solution_method: Read initial magnitude (0 dB/dec, 20 dB → DC gain=10). Zero at ω=1 (+20 dB/dec change). Pole at ω=10 (-20 dB/dec restores to 0). Double pole at ω=30 (-40 dB/dec). Assemble: TF = K(1+s/1) / [(1+s/10)(1+s/30)²] and find K from initial 20 dB.

notes: MM=120, 4 questions. Q2(a) OLTF has a RHP pole at s=4 making it a conditionally stable / non-minimum phase Nyquist problem. Q3 block diagram has an inner feedback loop (2/s) and PI-like controller K(1+a/s). Q4(i) is type-2 system requiring semi-log graph sheet. Marks split: Q1[10+4+6+10]=30, Q2[25+5]=30, Q3[12+5+5+8]=30, Q4[25+5]=30.
